frameworks.name: intuitive physics engine
frameworks.one_line: Brain runs a generative simulator of physical world dynamics

frameworks.name: forward simulation / mental simulation
frameworks.one_line: Predicts future states by rolling the world model forward

frameworks.name: physics network (parietal-frontal)
frameworks.one_line: Localized cortical substrate for intuitive physical inference

core_question: Does the brain's physics network actually run forward simulations to predict future physical states?
key_assumptions: Abstract representation of contact is needed to predict object fates
Physical scene properties can be decoded from PN activity
Predictive content is dissociable from low-level visual content
manipulations: Contrast scenes where two objects are vs. are not in contact
Decode predicted future collision events from current scene
Compare physics network against other visual areas
key_findings: PN abstractly represents whether two objects are currently in contact
PN carries abstract information about predicted future collisions
Other visual areas do not carry this predictive collision information
interpretations: PN contains a generative model of the physical world that conducts forward simulations
PN functions as the brain's 'physics engine' for prediction
